title: Art of Balling, The
description: Art of Balling, The is a book by Foreign Boy Jay. The Wisconsin prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime.
descriptionParagraphs: Art of Balling, The is a book by Foreign Boy Jay. The Wisconsin prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime.

Its interest lies partly in the way literary or informational writing gets collapsed into a public-morality problem. As a book, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Wisconsin. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: The Wisconsin prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime. The exported reason says: DOC 309.04 (4). More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: Art of Balling, The is a book by Foreign Boy Jay. The Wisconsin prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime.
banningParagraphs: Art of Balling, The entered censorship debates as a book associated with circulation politics, institutional control, morality, print scandal, risk knowledge, and sexuality. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around obscenity, public morality, incitement to violence, instructional harm, public order, and violence.

The earliest event currently captured here is Date not yet pinned down in Wisconsin, where Wisconsin corrections agencies and prison mailrooms excluded from prison circulation. The Wisconsin prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime. The exported reason says: DOC 309.04 (4). The Wisconsin prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime. The exported reason says: DOC 309.04 (4) (c ) 8. Pornography
The record already stretches across Wisconsin, which is why the page should be read as a cross-border censorship trail rather than a single isolated dispute.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: Literature Suppressed on Sexual Grounds
counterReadings.author: Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: Surveys the legal and moral language used to suppress books as obscene.
